data.body:
PASS 1 review — T10 (Infrastructure / IaC) [overlay] — on the kill-switch/disable-config + rollout-ordering FOLLOW-ON plan: NOT APPLICABLE / PASS. No findings.

This plan does not provision or configure infrastructure: no cloud resources, no IaC (Terraform/CloudFormation/CDK/Pulumi/Ansible), no Kubernetes/Helm. Its deliverables are (1) a project-level disable-the-gate config preference — already shipped as `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` (src/rebar/config.py:260, default off; src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:651,661-662), an application config key, not IaC; (2) a written rollback/back-out runbook + deploy-ordering for the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar / SIGNATURE events — software event-schema coexistence (the preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout in src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:44 and docs/event-schema.md), a documentation/process artifact, not infra provisioning; (3) a spec cross-reference. None of T10's binary checks engage — there is no remote-state/locking discipline, IAM policy, idempotency/drift surface, blast-radius/destroy-replace, plaintext-secret, cost/sizing, or new-infra observability concern in scope.

Per the T10 overlay gate ("apply only when the plan provisions or configures infrastructure ... else PASS not-applicable") and its ANTI-FP rule (not-applicable for non-infra tickets), T10 PASSES as not-applicable. Note for disambiguation: "deploy ordering" here is software event-log rollout sequencing (rightly the province of T12), not infra deploy/apply ordering — so it does not pull this ticket into T10 scope.

Affirmations: T10 — not-applicable (no infrastructure provisioning or configuration in scope).
